enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hosted ActivityPub microblogging server built from scratch in .NET for performance and maintainability.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Iceshrimp.NET is a complete rewrite of the earlier Iceshrimp-JS project, built on a new .NET backend with a Blazor WASM frontend. It is a decentralized, self-hosted microblogging server that speaks ActivityPub, letting an instance federate with Mastodon, Misskey, Akkoma and the wider fediverse.

The project positions itself as a community-centric alternative to the larger ActivityPub server projects, with a stated focus on performance, resource efficiency and long-term maintainability rather than rapid feature sprawl. It ships a Mastodon-compatible API so existing third-party apps such as Elk, Tusky and Ice Cubes can connect to an instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Server administrators who already run or want to run their own fediverse instance and prefer a lighter, more efficient backend than existing ActivityPub servers, plus users who want to migrate an existing Iceshrimp-JS community onto a more maintainable codebase.
The project explicitly does not run a flagship public instance, so it's aimed at operators willing to self-host rather than end users looking for a ready-made account to sign up to.

features.title: ActivityPub federation
features.description: Interoperates with Mastodon, Misskey, Akkoma and other fediverse servers over the open ActivityPub standard.

features.title: Mastodon-compatible API
features.description: Supports many existing Mastodon-client apps (Elk, Tusky, Ice Cubes and others) without requiring a dedicated client.

features.title: .NET backend
features.description: Rewritten from the ground up in C# for improved performance and resource efficiency compared to the original Iceshrimp-JS.

features.title: Blazor WASM frontend
features.description: Web client built with Blazor WebAssembly, served directly from the instance.

features.title: Configurable public preview
features.description: Instance operators can configure what an anonymous HTML preview of posts looks like to non-federated visitors and crawlers.

features.title: Migration tooling
features.description: Provides a path for existing Iceshrimp-JS instances to migrate their data to Iceshrimp.NET.

pros: Free, open-source and self-hosted with no vendor lock-in
Performance-focused rewrite aimed at lower resource usage
Federates with the broader ActivityPub fediverse
Compatible with popular existing Mastodon client apps
Documented migration path from Iceshrimp-JS
cons: Still beta software with no official flagship instance to try first
Requires self-hosting and server administration skills
Smaller ecosystem and community than Mastodon or Misskey

faqs.question: Is Iceshrimp.NET free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is fully open source and free to self-host.

faqs.question: Can I use my existing Mastodon app with Iceshrimp.NET?
faqs.answer: Yes, it exposes a Mastodon-compatible API, so apps like Elk, Tusky and Ice Cubes can connect to an instance.

faqs.question: Is there an official public instance to join?
faqs.answer: No, the project does not operate a flagship instance; you need to self-host or find a community-run instance.

faqs.question: What is Iceshrimp.NET a rewrite of?
faqs.answer: It is a from-scratch rewrite of the earlier Iceshrimp-JS project, moving the backend to .NET.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted fediverse instance
use_cases.description: Run a personal or community ActivityPub server without depending on a third-party host.

use_cases.title: Migrating from Iceshrimp-JS
use_cases.description: Move an existing Iceshrimp-JS community onto a more performant, maintainable backend.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Iceshrimp.NET is a free, open-source ActivityPub server rewritten in .NET, offering Mastodon-compatible fediverse federation for self-hosters.

enrichment.short_description: Lightweight, self-hosted comment server written in Python, built as a privacy-friendly alternative to Disqus.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Isso is a small, self-hosted commenting server that site owners embed into blogs and articles instead of using a third-party service like Disqus. The server is written in Python and stores comments in a SQLite database, keeping the deployment footprint small.
The embeddable client is a single JavaScript file (around 65KB, 20KB gzipped), which keeps page-weight impact low compared to many commercial commenting widgets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Bloggers and site operators who want comments on their static or dynamic sites without sending visitor data to a third-party ad-supported platform, and who are comfortable self-hosting a small Python service.

features.title: Markdown comments
features.description: Visitors can format comments using Markdown.

features.title: Self-edit and delete window
features.description: Commenters can edit or delete their own comment within a configurable time window (15 minutes by default).

features.title: Moderation queue
features.description: New comments can be held for moderator approval before appearing publicly.

features.title: Disqus and WordPress import
features.description: Existing comment threads can be migrated in from Disqus or WordPress.

features.title: Minimal JS client
features.description: A single small JavaScript file embeds the comment widget on any page.

features.title: SQLite storage
features.description: No separate database server is required; comments are stored in a SQLite file.

pros: Free and fully open source
Very small JavaScript footprint on embedding pages
No external database dependency to manage
Straightforward migration from Disqus or WordPress comments
Keeps visitor comment data off third-party platforms
cons: Requires self-hosting a Python service, not a plug-and-play SaaS
Fewer built-in integrations than commercial commenting platforms
No official managed hosting option

faqs.question: Is Isso free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Isso is open-source software and free to self-host.

faqs.question: What database does Isso use?
faqs.answer: Isso stores comments in a local SQLite database, so no separate database server is needed.

faqs.question: Can I migrate comments from Disqus?
faqs.answer: Yes, Isso provides import support for existing Disqus and WordPress comment data.

faqs.question: What browsers does the comment widget support?
faqs.answer: The embeddable JavaScript client supports Firefox, Safari, Chrome and Edge.

use_cases.title: Blog comments without third-party tracking
use_cases.description: Add a comment section to a personal or company blog while keeping visitor data on your own server.

use_cases.title: Replacing Disqus
use_cases.description: Migrate an existing Disqus-powered comment section to a self-hosted alternative.
use_cases.audience: Site owners moving away from ad-supported comment platforms

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Isso is a free, open-source, self-hosted commenting server for blogs, offering Markdown comments and a lightweight JavaScript embed.

enrichment.short_description: Modern, self-hosted forum platform built with Django and React, designed to be fast, scalable and customizable.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Misago is an open-source forum application built on Django with a React frontend, started by developer Rafał Pitoń in 2012. It aims to be a modern alternative to older PHP forum software, with Docker-based deployment, a plugin-friendly admin control panel, and a responsive interface.
The project is actively maintained, with ongoing releases and a community forum where translation, styling and plugin discussions happen alongside support threads.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Community managers and developers who want a self-hosted discussion forum with a modern JavaScript frontend and are comfortable deploying and customizing a Django application, typically via Docker.

features.title: Docker deployment
features.description: Ships with Docker support for streamlined installation and updates.

features.title: Admin control panel
features.description: Web-based ACP for managing users, permissions and forum settings.

features.title: Theming and branding
features.description: Custom styling and page header/branding options.

features.title: Plugin architecture
features.description: Extensible via plugins for adding functionality beyond the core.

features.title: User permission system
features.description: Granular permission groups control what members can see and do.

features.title: Internationalization
features.description: Interface has been translated into languages beyond English, including German.

pros: Free and open source with an active GitHub project
Modern React-based frontend rather than legacy PHP templating
Docker support simplifies self-hosted deployment
Plugin system for extending functionality
Active community forum for support and translations
cons: Requires comfort running a Django/Python stack to self-host
Smaller plugin ecosystem than long-established forum software like phpBB or SMF
No official managed/hosted version

faqs.question: Is Misago free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Misago is open-source software, free to download and self-host.

faqs.question: What tech stack does Misago use?
faqs.answer: A Django (Python) backend with a React frontend.

faqs.question: Can I deploy Misago with Docker?
faqs.answer: Yes, the project supports Docker-based deployment.

faqs.question: Does Misago support plugins?
faqs.answer: Yes, it has a plugin architecture for extending the admin control panel and forum functionality.

use_cases.title: Community discussion forum
use_cases.description: Run a self-hosted forum for a community, product or hobby group with a modern web interface.

use_cases.title: Customizable branded forum
use_cases.description: Build a forum with custom theming and plugins tailored to a specific organization.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Misago is a free, open-source, self-hosted forum platform built with Django and React, featuring Docker deployment and a plugin system.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source XMPP-based social platform combining chat, communities, blogs and video calls in one self-hostable app.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Movim, started by Timothée Jaussoin in 2010, is a web-based social and communication platform built on the open XMPP protocol rather than a proprietary backend. It combines one-to-one and group chat, community 'Spaces', blog-style posts, 24-hour disappearing stories, and audio/video calling in a single progressive web app.
Because it's built on XMPP, a Movim account can interoperate with other XMPP clients such as Conversations and Dino, and instances can be run on standard XMPP servers like ejabberd or Prosody.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Privacy-conscious individuals, communities, classrooms and organizations that want a decentralized alternative to mainstream social platforms and are comfortable running or joining an XMPP-based server.

features.title: End-to-end encrypted chat
features.description: Supports OMEMO encryption for private one-to-one messaging.

features.title: Communities and Spaces
features.description: Group discussion areas for organizing communities around topics.

features.title: Blog publishing
features.description: Users can publish blog-style posts with a real-time feed.

features.title: Disappearing stories
features.description: Photo/text stories that expire after 24 hours.

features.title: Audio/video calling
features.description: One-to-one and group video calls with screen sharing.

features.title: XMPP interoperability
features.description: Works alongside other XMPP clients like Conversations and Dino, and runs on standard servers such as ejabberd or Prosody.

pros: Free and open source, built on an open decentralized protocol
Combines chat, blogging, communities and video calls in one app
Interoperates with the wider XMPP client ecosystem
Progressive web app installs on phones, tablets and desktops
Supports end-to-end encrypted messaging via OMEMO
cons: Requires an XMPP server (ejabberd/Prosody) to self-host
Smaller mainstream user base than commercial social networks
Project relies on donations for ongoing hosting and development

faqs.question: Is Movim free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Movim is free and open-source software; the project accepts optional donations to fund hosting and development.

faqs.question: What protocol does Movim use?
faqs.answer: It's built on XMPP, an open, decentralized messaging standard.

faqs.question: Can I use Movim with other XMPP clients?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Movim account works with other XMPP clients such as Conversations and Dino.

faqs.question: Does Movim support video calls?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports one-to-one and group video calls with screen sharing.

use_cases.title: Decentralized community platform
use_cases.description: Run a self-hosted social space for a community without relying on a corporate-controlled network.

use_cases.title: Unified XMPP client
use_cases.description: Use Movim as a full-featured web client for an existing XMPP/Jabber account.
use_cases.audience: XMPP users wanting chat, blogging and calls in one interface

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Movim is a free, open-source XMPP-based social platform with chat, communities, blogs and video calls, self-hostable on your own server.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source PHP script for building self-hosted social networking sites, communities and membership platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: OSSN (Open Source Social Network) is a PHP-based script that lets operators stand up their own branded social networking site, online community, membership platform or private collaboration space. It's distributed as free, open-source software with no artificial user limits or licensing fees for the core product.

The project provides user profiles, timelines, groups, messaging, notifications and photo albums out of the box, and can be extended through a component marketplace of add-ons and a hook/event system that avoids modifying core files.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Community builders, membership organizations, educational institutions, alumni associations and enterprises that want to run their own social network on infrastructure they control, rather than building on a third-party social platform.

features.title: User profiles and timelines
features.description: Chronological activity timelines and customizable member profiles.

features.title: Groups and messaging
features.description: Group spaces plus private messaging and notifications between members.

features.title: Photo albums
features.description: Members can upload and organize photo albums.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Production-ready REST APIs for building integrations or custom clients.

features.title: Component marketplace
features.description: Hundreds of add-ons available to extend the base platform.

features.title: Redis caching support
features.description: Can be optimized with Redis and caching for performance at scale.

pros: Free, open-source core with no per-user licensing fees
Extensible via a large component/add-on marketplace
Full data ownership since it runs on your own infrastructure
Mobile-responsive out of the box
REST API available for custom integrations
cons: Requires self-hosting a PHP application and its infrastructure
UI conventions feel dated compared to newer social platforms
Some add-ons and support options are paid rather than included

faqs.question: Is OSSN free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core OSSN script is free and open source; some add-ons and managed hosting options are offered separately.

faqs.question: What language is OSSN written in?
faqs.answer: PHP.

faqs.question: Can I extend OSSN with plugins?
faqs.answer: Yes, it has a component marketplace and a hook/event system for extending functionality without modifying core files.

faqs.question: Does OSSN have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, it provides REST APIs for integrations and custom clients.

use_cases.title: Niche community site
use_cases.description: Launch a dedicated social network for a hobby, interest group or fan community.

use_cases.title: Internal collaboration network
use_cases.description: Run a private, self-hosted social space for an organization or membership group.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: OSSN is a free, open-source PHP script for building self-hosted social networks, communities and membership platforms with full data ownership.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, federated link-aggregator and forum platform, an ActivityPub-based alternative to Reddit and Lemmy.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: PieFed is an open-source link aggregator and forum platform created by Rimu Atkinson, with its first commit made in July 2023. Written in Python with Flask, it federates over ActivityPub, sharing posts, votes and comments with Lemmy, Mbin and other fediverse platforms, making it directly interoperable with those existing communities.

The project is licensed under AGPL-3.0 and is positioned as a community-governed, ad-free alternative to centralized link-aggregation sites like Reddit, run either on the flagship piefed.social instance or self-hosted independently.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Communities and individuals who want a Reddit-style discussion and link-sharing platform without corporate ownership, algorithmic feeds or advertising, and who value interoperability with the wider Lemmy/Mbin fediverse.

features.title: ActivityPub federation
features.description: Interoperates with Lemmy, Mbin and other fediverse platforms, sharing posts, votes and comments across servers.

features.title: No ads or tracking
features.description: The platform does not run advertising or engagement-optimization algorithms.

features.title: User-built feeds
features.description: Feeds are curated by communities rather than an opaque recommendation algorithm.

features.title: Mobile app compatibility
features.description: Works with existing Lemmy-compatible mobile apps via its federated API.

features.title: Self-hostable
features.description: Documented installation process (INSTALL.md) for running your own instance.

features.title: Python/Flask codebase
features.description: Open, developer-friendly codebase for contributions and customization.

pros: Free and open source under AGPL-3.0
Federates with Lemmy and Mbin, extending reach beyond a single instance
No advertising or engagement-manipulation algorithms
Can join an existing instance or self-host independently
Compatible with existing Lemmy mobile apps
cons: Smaller user base than Reddit or established Lemmy instances
Self-hosting requires Python/Flask server administration skills
Federated moderation and content policies vary by instance

faqs.question: Is PieFed free?
faqs.answer: Yes, PieFed is free, open-source software licensed under AGPL-3.0.

faqs.question: Is PieFed compatible with Lemmy?
faqs.answer: Yes, it federates over ActivityPub and shares posts, votes and comments with Lemmy and Mbin instances.

faqs.question: Can I self-host PieFed?
faqs.answer: Yes, the project provides installation documentation for running your own instance.

faqs.question: Who created PieFed?
faqs.answer: PieFed was created by Rimu Atkinson, with development starting in mid-2023.

use_cases.title: Ad-free link aggregation community
use_cases.description: Join or host a Reddit-style discussion community without ads or algorithmic feeds.
use_cases.audience: Communities seeking a decentralized Reddit alternative

use_cases.title: Federated forum for a niche topic
use_cases.description: Run a self-hosted instance focused on a specific topic that still interoperates with the broader fediverse.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: PieFed is a free, open-source, ActivityPub-federated link aggregator and forum platform, an alternative to Reddit and Lemmy.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source Ruby on Rails Q&A software that powers the Codidact network of community knowledge-sharing sites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: QPixel is the open-source, Ruby on Rails-based software that powers Codidact, a community-run network of question-and-answer sites covering topics like software development, writing, and more. It's built and maintained by the Codidact community itself and is licensed under AGPL.

The project can be self-hosted independently of Codidact's own network; the GitHub repository includes setup instructions for a local instance, and community-contributed Docker configuration is available for containerized deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Communities and developers who want to run their own Stack Exchange-style Q&A site under their own governance, or who want to contribute to or study the software behind the Codidact network.

features.title: Question-and-answer format
features.description: Community knowledge-sharing structured as questions, answers and voting, similar to Stack Exchange-style sites.

features.title: Multi-community network support
features.description: Powers the multiple distinct topical communities that make up the Codidact network.

features.title: Docker deployment option
features.description: Community-contributed Docker configuration supports containerized self-hosting.

features.title: AGPL licensing
features.description: Fully open-source under the AGPL, with source available on GitHub.

features.title: Ruby on Rails codebase
features.description: Built on Rails, with an active develop branch for contributions.

pros: Free and fully open source under AGPL
Powers a real, active production network (Codidact)
Can be self-hosted independently of Codidact.com
Active open-source contributor community and documented setup process
cons: Primarily documented for the Codidact use case rather than as a generic drop-in product
Self-hosting requires Ruby on Rails deployment experience
Smaller ecosystem than mainstream Q&A platforms like Stack Exchange

faqs.question: Is qpixel free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is open-source software licensed under AGPL.

faqs.question: What does qpixel power?
faqs.answer: It's the software behind the Codidact network of community Q&A sites.

faqs.question: Can I self-host qpixel outside of Codidact?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GitHub repository documents how to run a local or Docker-based instance.

faqs.question: What is qpixel built with?
faqs.answer: Ruby on Rails.

use_cases.title: Running a community Q&A site
use_cases.description: Self-host a Stack Exchange-style question-and-answer community for a specific topic.

use_cases.title: Contributing to Codidact
use_cases.description: Develop features for or study the codebase behind the existing Codidact network.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: qpixel is the free, open-source Ruby on Rails Q&A platform that powers Codidact, self-hostable for your own community knowledge base.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted, privacy-focused commenting engine written in Go, with social login, moderation and no external tracking.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Remark42, created by developer Umputun, is a self-hosted comment engine written in Go, built as a lightweight alternative to Disqus that doesn't track or profile visitors. It embeds into blogs, articles or any page needing a comment section, and stores everything in a single embedded data file with no external database required.

It supports social login through Google, Facebook, Microsoft, GitHub, Apple, Yandex, Patreon, Discord, Telegram and custom OAuth2 providers, alongside optional anonymous commenting, and can manage multiple sites from one instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Bloggers, publishers and site operators who want a full-featured comment system with moderation and voting, but without sending reader data to a third-party advertising-funded platform.

features.title: Nested comment threads
features.description: Multi-level nested comments with both tree and plain display modes.

features.title: Social login
features.description: Sign-in via Google, Facebook, Microsoft, GitHub, Apple, Yandex, Patreon, Discord, Telegram or custom OAuth2.

features.title: Markdown editor
features.description: Comment composer with a Markdown formatting toolbar.

features.title: Image uploads
features.description: Drag-and-drop image uploads directly in comments.

features.title: Moderation tools
features.description: Remove comments, block users, and manage a voting and pinning system.

features.title: Notification channels
features.description: Sends notifications via Telegram, Slack, email or webhooks.

features.title: No external database
features.description: All data is stored in a single embedded data file, simplifying backup and deployment.

pros: Free and open source with no licensing costs
No external database to provision or manage
Privacy-focused: minimal data collection, encrypted/hashed storage
Runs as a single Docker container or standalone binary on Linux, Windows or macOS
Supports many social login providers
cons: Requires self-hosting and basic server administration
No managed/hosted SaaS version offered by the project
Fewer built-in analytics/reporting features than commercial comment platforms

faqs.question: Is remark42 free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is open-source and free to self-host.

faqs.question: What language is remark42 written in?
faqs.answer: Go.

faqs.question: Does remark42 need a separate database?
faqs.answer: No, it stores everything in a single embedded data file.

faqs.question: Can users comment anonymously?
faqs.answer: Yes, anonymous commenting can be enabled alongside social login options.

use_cases.title: Privacy-respecting blog comments
use_cases.description: Add a full-featured comment section to a blog without third-party ad-network tracking.

use_cases.title: Multi-site comment hosting
use_cases.description: Manage comments for several websites from a single self-hosted instance.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Remark42 is a free, open-source, self-hosted commenting engine written in Go, offering social login, moderation and no external tracking.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted Q&A platform and knowledge base for teams, free as open source with paid Pro and Cloud tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Scoold is a self-hosted Q&A platform, forum and knowledge base that teams use to capture and organize internal knowledge. The open-source Community edition is free under Apache 2.0 and can be deployed as a Docker container, standalone JAR, or native executable on any cloud provider or private infrastructure, keeping data on-premises.

Beyond the free edition, Scoold sells one-time-fee 'Pro' and 'Pro + Source' licenses that add enterprise SSO, file uploads, chat-tool integrations and unlimited spaces, plus a subscription-based 'Scoold Cloud' managed-hosting option. The project reports over 1,000 deployed instances and more than 5 million Docker Hub downloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Teams and organizations that want an internal knowledge base, developer Q&A hub, support center or issue tracker with data kept fully on their own infrastructure, and that need enterprise features like SAML SSO once they outgrow the free tier.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Search across all questions, answers and comments.

features.title: Enterprise SSO
features.description: SAML 2.0, LDAP, OAuth 2.0 and SCIM user provisioning available on Pro tiers.

features.title: Gamification
features.description: Reputation points and badges to encourage participation.

features.title: REST API
features.description: API access for building integrations and custom tooling.

features.title: Multiple spaces
features.description: Isolated spaces/sub-communities within a single instance.

features.title: Chat tool integrations
features.description: Connects with Slack, Microsoft Teams, Mattermost, Zapier and n8n.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Runs as a Docker container, standalone JAR, or native executable on any cloud or on-premises.

pros: Free, open-source Community edition under Apache 2.0
Data stays fully on-premises when self-hosted
Enterprise SSO and SCIM available for organizations that need it
Flexible deployment options (Docker, JAR, native executable)
Long track record with 5M+ Docker Hub downloads
cons: Enterprise SSO, file uploads and unlimited spaces require a paid Pro license
Pro licenses are one-time fees with only 12 months of included updates/support
Cloud (managed hosting) pricing isn't published on the pricing page

faqs.question: Is Scoold free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community edition is free and open source under Apache 2.0. Paid Pro and Cloud tiers add enterprise features and managed hosting.

faqs.question: How much does Scoold Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Scoold Pro is a one-time fee of €499, and Pro + Source (with full source code access) is €1,999, per the official pricing page.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Scoold?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be deployed via Docker, a standalone JAR, or a native executable on any cloud provider or private infrastructure.

faqs.question: Does Scoold integrate with Slack or Teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pro tiers include integrations with Slack, Microsoft Teams, Mattermost, Zapier and n8n.

use_cases.title: Internal team knowledge base
use_cases.description: Capture and organize institutional knowledge with a searchable Q&A format.
use_cases.audience: Internal IT, engineering and support teams

use_cases.title: Developer support hub
use_cases.description: Run a self-hosted Q&A community for a product's developers or users.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Scoold is a self-hosted Q&A platform and knowledge base for teams, free as open source with paid Pro licenses and a managed Cloud option.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source PHP and MySQL forum software for running a self-hosted online community.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Simple Machines Forum (SMF) is a free, open-source community forum package written in PHP, with its first branded release (SMF 1.0 Beta 1a) shipping in September 2003 as a rewrite of the earlier YaBB SE project. Versions 2.0 and later are licensed under the 3-clause BSD license.

It supports MySQL, PostgreSQL and SQLite, includes a Package Manager for installing hundreds of community-built modifications and themes, and is designed to run with a light footprint on shared or self-managed hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Community operators who want a traditional, feature-rich bulletin-board style forum with a large library of existing themes and modifications, and who are comfortable self-hosting a PHP/MySQL application.

features.title: Package Manager
features.description: Install community-built modifications and themes directly from within the admin panel.

features.title: Permission and user group management
features.description: Fine-grained control over what different member groups can see and do.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Can run multiple language packs simultaneously on the same forum.

features.title: Unread topic tracking
features.description: Tracks new and unread topics beyond just a user's last visit.

features.title: Multiple database support
features.description: Works with MySQL, PostgreSQL or SQLite.

features.title: Multi-format output
features.description: Supports XHTML, XML, RSS and WAP output formats.

pros: Free and open source under the 3-clause BSD license (v2.0+)
Large, mature ecosystem of themes and modifications via the Package Manager
Runs efficiently on modest shared hosting
Supports multiple database backends
Long track record dating back over two decades
cons: Interface conventions feel dated compared to newer forum software
Requires self-managed PHP/MySQL hosting and maintenance
No official managed/hosted SaaS version

faqs.question: Is Simple Machines Forum free?
faqs.answer: Yes, SMF is free, open-source software.

faqs.question: What license does SMF use?
faqs.answer: Versions 2.0 and 2.1 are licensed under the 3-clause BSD license.

faqs.question: What databases does SMF support?
faqs.answer: MySQL, PostgreSQL and SQLite.

faqs.question: Can I extend SMF with plugins?
faqs.answer: Yes, the built-in Package Manager lets you install community modifications and themes.

use_cases.title: Traditional online community forum
use_cases.description: Stand up a classic bulletin-board style forum for a hobby, brand or interest community.

use_cases.title: Migrating from legacy forum software
use_cases.description: Move an existing PHP-based forum to a maintained, actively developed codebase.
use_cases.audience: Site owners with established forum communities

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Simple Machines Forum (SMF) is free, open-source PHP and MySQL forum software for running a self-hosted online community.
